rationale: The citing paper uses Bowman and Narayandas (2001) in its model-development section to justify key relationship antecedents included in the purchase timing and quantity framework. Beyond a general literature list mention, the citation is tied to a specific construct choice: cross-buying as an indicator of switching costs and recurrent needs (also reflected in Table 1 rationale). This indicates a functional role in the conceptual setup of the CLV model rather than a purely perfunctory citation.
relation_description: The citing study draws on Bowman and Narayandas (2001) as prior empirical grounding for relationship-marketing antecedents used in its CLV modeling framework, especially the logic for including cross-buying and related customer-firm interaction factors. Thus, the cited paper supports the conceptual foundation for variable selection and model framing.
